new_terms.context: A mechanism used by ERCOT to calculate real-time scarcity price adders.
An additional price component in ERCOT's energy-only market during tight supply.
Power plants that generally run only when there is high demand.

new_terms.suggested_translation: 风险折减 / 估值折扣
new_terms.context: A deliberate reduction or discount applied to forecasts or asset values to account for risk.
The ability of the electric system to supply the aggregate electrical demand and energy requirements at all times.

new_terms.suggested_translation: 欠压供电 / 局部限电
new_terms.context: A drop in voltage in an electrical power supply system, often used to prevent a total blackout.
The predetermined price for an energy contract to be delivered in the future.
